cards.front: What is the meaning of \forall (for all) in mathematical logic?
cards.back: A universal quantifier used to describe a statement that is true for all elements in a set

cards.front: What is the meaning of \exists (there exists) in mathematical logic?
cards.back: An existential quantifier used to describe a statement that is true for at least one element in a set

cards.front: How is the quantifier \forall used in statements?
cards.back: The quantifier \forall is used to describe a statement that applies to every element in a set, e.g. \forall x P(x)

cards.front: How is the quantifier \exists used in statements?
cards.back: The quantifier \exists is used to describe a statement that applies to at least one element in a set, e.g. \exists x P(x)

cards.front: What is the difference between \forall and \exists in mathematical logic?
cards.back: \forall is used to describe a statement that is true for all elements in a set, while \exists is used to describe a statement that is true for at least one element in a set
